title: LifeOps + Hermes
shortOutcome: A private operating system that turns daily conversations into routed, durable context without surrendering human control.
role: System designer, integrator, and daily operator
ownership: I designed the LifeOps information architecture, authority boundaries, Telegram topic lanes, personality layer, skills, and automations. Hermes/OpenClaw is an upstream agent framework that I configure and extend rather than claim as original work.

evidence.label: Operational use
evidence.detail: The system is the working interface for active priorities, projects, reference material, and repeatable workflows.
evidence.limitation: Personal records remain private; this case study uses a sanitized architecture and no private messages.

evidence.label: Routing model
evidence.detail: Strategy, plans, weekly execution, active pipelines, offers, and workflows each have an explicit canonical home.

evidence.label: Write safety
evidence.detail: Update the existing owner, then weekly context, then inbox, and only create a file when no canonical home exists.

problem.title: A second brain fails when capture creates more maintenance.
body: Projects, people, ideas, client work, audio operations, and career decisions arrive through different tools and at different speeds. A conventional notes vault stores information, but it still depends on consistent manual filing and retrieval.
LifeOps gives the agent a controlled way to read current context, route new facts, and update existing sources of truth from the conversational surface I already use every day.

ownership.title: An upstream agent framework, heavily shaped into a personal operating layer.
body: Hermes/OpenClaw supplies the general agent runtime. I built the working system around it: Telegram topic lanes, personality and response conventions, vault maps, memory boundaries, project skills, action permissions, and automations such as audio delivery and market execution.
The interesting engineering is the governance layer—deciding what the agent may read, where it may write, when it must ask, and which file remains authoritative after a conversation ends.

systemMap.label: Telegram lanes
systemMap.detail: Focused conversations separate projects and operational contexts.

systemMap.label: Files + services
systemMap.detail: Targeted project records, workflows, and tool state are read only when relevant.

systemMap.label: Current board
systemMap.detail: The weekly execution layer holds live priorities and next actions.

systemMap.label: Authority contract
systemMap.detail: Read/write lanes and permissions determine what the agent may change.

systemMap.label: Canonical router
systemMap.detail: Existing owner first; weekly board second; inbox if uncertain; new file last.

systemMap.label: Durable update
systemMap.detail: A fact, task, decision, or workflow lands in its maintained home.

systemMap.label: Escalation
systemMap.detail: Structural edits, conflicts, and sensitive actions return to human review.

decisions.title: Pointers before bulk context
decisions.decision: The agent starts with indexes, the current weekly board, and targeted files instead of loading the vault.
decisions.consequence: Responses stay grounded while reducing irrelevant context and accidental disclosure.

decisions.title: Existing truth before new documents
decisions.decision: The write router updates the file that already owns a fact before creating anything new.
decisions.consequence: The system resists the note sprawl that makes many second brains harder to trust over time.

decisions.title: Conversation is an interface, not the database
decisions.decision: Durable decisions are routed into explicit files with authority boundaries.
decisions.consequence: The system remains inspectable and useful outside any single agent session.

buildRecord.label: 01 / MAP
buildRecord.title: Canonical vault architecture
buildRecord.detail: Strategy, execution, pipeline, offers, workflows, and inbox each have defined responsibility.

buildRecord.label: 02 / GOVERN
buildRecord.title: Agent authority contract
buildRecord.detail: Safe reads, write order, escalation points, permissions, and anti-sprawl rules are documented.

buildRecord.label: 03 / INTERFACE
buildRecord.title: Telegram topic lanes
buildRecord.detail: Daily use is separated into focused contexts while sharing the same controlled operating layer.

buildRecord.label: 04 / EXTEND
buildRecord.title: Skills and automations
buildRecord.detail: Reusable tools connect conversations to real workflows instead of stopping at advice.

failures.title: Agents tend to create a new note for every thought
failures.observed: Unconstrained capture duplicated facts and weakened the source-of-truth model.
failures.response: Added an explicit routing order and anti-sprawl rule that makes new files the last option.

failures.title: Personal context cannot become portfolio media
failures.observed: The strongest evidence contains private people, plans, financial details, and conversations.
failures.response: Show architecture, redacted field shapes, and synthetic examples—not screenshots of the live vault.

failures.title: Framework upgrades can disturb custom behavior
failures.observed: Personality, skills, and routing rules sit on top of an evolving upstream runtime.
failures.response: Keep custom contracts in inspectable files and test critical lanes after upgrades.

currentStatus.title: Private, active, and used daily.
body: LifeOps is not a public SaaS product. It is an operating system under continuous use, with new skills and workflow connections added when a repeated real-world need earns them.
